title: JavaScript Concepts
response:
The Date object handles dates and times in JavaScript.

Creating dates:
• new Date() — current date/time.
• new Date('2025-06-15') — from ISO string.
• new Date(year, month, day, ...) — month is 0-indexed (0 = January).
• Date.now() — current timestamp in milliseconds.

Common methods:
• .getFullYear(), .getMonth() (0-indexed!), .getDate(), .getDay()
• .getHours(), .getMinutes(), .getSeconds()
• .toISOString(), .toLocaleDateString(), .toLocaleTimeString()

Date arithmetic: subtract two Date objects to get milliseconds difference.

Note: For complex date operations, use libraries like dayjs or date-fns.
